reviews.review_description: Please ignore the low ratings. I played this in early 2026 and it was one of the very best gaming experiences I have had on any platform. I'm finished and never had any game breaking bugs or crashes. Frame rate stayed smooth almost always even in some heavy fights while playing on Quest 3S. Only complaint is low textures when looking in some mirrors and in a few places here and there in the world, but what they have accomplished on standalone is nothing short of magical. Don't let the first hour or so set all your expectations because once it opens up and let's you choose where to go it gets so much better. Best game physics in VR I have ever tried. You can interact with and/or manipulate most of what you can see and whatever items you leave will still be where you left it in every time and place you go. Story is top notch, as is the voice acting. The puzzles are also amazing and many can be solved in multiple ways allowing you to get creative and feel like a genius when solving. I watched Wolf in VR play a lot of this game after I finished and he completed many puzzles differently than I did. I would recommend at full price but please everyone don't miss this if you see a sale or have been considering buying it. Also those who played it many months ago or near release please give it another chance now. Let's get this rating closer to what it deserves. It should be nothing less than 4.7 out of 5. I challenge all players on the Meta store to find another game like this. Wish we had more games like it.

reviews.review_title: A year later - SO MUCH IMPROVEMENT
reviews.review_description:
It’s exactly 1 year since I refunded this game, as it was a mess, I picked it up again yesterday and wow so much improvement so have adjusted my score, looks stunning now and plays so much better, I’m buzzing to go back in.
More updates as I go further.
I have now completed the game & yes there were lots of issues, objects stuck in walls, some crashes but I absolutely loved the story and gameplay, complicated at times, jumping back and forth, but do not regret buying it second time around.
If you can get it in a sale jump in and enjoy one of the best story adventures on Quest.

I’m 8 hours in the game and it’s great so far and definitely recommend because there is nothing like this in the vr industry and its on quest 3. But there are some bugs but there are a small team and you have to expect to see bugs in a 15 hour game, one more thing is the mirrors in other area they don’t load properly but beside some small glitch it runs good the loading is fast and it’s amazing.👍👍👍👍

(Quest 3 review)
Remember it’s a Mobile headset so it won’t run like the ps5 version which is even better omg 😱

I think this game is great! It’s such an adventure and there’s so much to explore and find secrets. I agree with others, ignore the bad reviews because this game is so much fun. It has a few little glitchy things going on, but nothing that keeps you from having a great experience and loads of fun. The graphics are so pretty there’s just so much to see. Fantastic story, I should say stories. Get it. All the work that went into this it’s worth the price. Cheers!

reviews.review_title: Not the best 50 dollars I’ve spent…
reviews.review_description:
I play on quest 3 and omg it had so many bugs I had to restart my game and continue about 10 times because I got stuck or just couldn’t find or finish a puzzle because I piece fell out the map. 

I don’t know if it’s just because I played on quest 3 but I personally wouldn’t recommend it on quest 3. Horrible experience. 1 star.

How can such a glichy game with an utterly broken combat system be one of the most enjoyable vr games I have ever played? Great story, very well thought out puzzles and mostly great graphics. Yes the glitches can be annoying at times but they are relatively few and fair between and generally don't spoil the enjoyment of the game.

This is a difficult review to leave, this game should be a five star game, but at times it barely reaches a three star rating due to issues, which I will endeavor to explain.  Firstly the positives, this is a very ambitious attempt at bringing this wonderful game to standalone android hardware, the story is engaging, confusing, creative and interesting, the puzzles are (not) complicated, but because they can be approached from multiple directions and in whichever order you choose, they take on an overly complex and satisfying nature. The collision detection is at times too good, which makes it very clumsy, expect things to go flying all over the place if you're not careful. Generally though it is a very fun gaming experience.  Now the bad stuff, objects completely disappearing (this is I feel tied to collision detection), Samuel's audio levels are all over the place (his dialogue is extremely important to the overall story, but at times he is barely audible, also his dialogue is seemingly not tied to the dialogue setting in the main settings) and the subtitles more often than not show completely different text to that which is being said. The height calibration in standing mode actually doesn't work and needs a complete revisit from the devs, I have been 7 feet tall and 2 feet tall and been thrown out of the game world due to being so low that I collided with the floor!  There are other minor issues too numerous to mention, but all will be shown during my playthough on my channel... I've given it 4 stars, but only because I can't give it 3 and a half, and it's better than 3... Still needs much work, but kudos to the devs for what they've achieved so far.

Wanderer on Meta Quest – A Brilliant, Underrated Masterpiece That Deserves a 5/5

This is the very first time I am writing a review, simply because I strongly disagree with the current score. Wanderer absolutely does not deserve a 3.4 , it deserves a solid 5 out of 5.

As a huge fan of story-driven VR puzzle and adventure games like Red Matter 1 & 2, The Walking Dead: Saints & Sinners, The House of Da Vinci, Metro, The Room, and Ghost Town, I can confidently say that Wanderer belongs at the very top of this genre.

From the very beginning, Wanderer impressed me with its outstanding visual design and atmosphere. The environments are not only beautiful, but incredibly detailed, expansive, and full of character. Every world feels handcrafted, alive, and meaningful. You’re not just moving from level to level — you’re traveling through carefully designed experiences that constantly surprise you.

What truly sets Wanderer apart is its ingenious mix of escape room mechanics, clever puzzles, and time-travel storytelling. The puzzles range from accessible to genuinely challenging, but they are always fair and well thought out. The game never feels frustrating; instead, it encourages you to think creatively and rewards curiosity. Solving puzzles feels deeply satisfying because everything is so smartly connected.

The variety of worlds is phenomenal. One moment you’re immersed in historical settings, the next you’re dealing with science fiction concepts, futuristic ideas, or unexpected twists often combined with a great sense of humor. This blend of history, sci-fi, and lighthearted moments keeps the experience fresh and engaging throughout. You never know what’s coming next, and that sense of discovery is magical.

I am constantly playing this game with a smile on my face. Wanderer manages to surprise me again and again, even when I think I know what to expect. The pacing is excellent, the storytelling is intriguing, and the overall structure feels intelligent and respectful of the player’s time and intelligence.

Yes, the game can be complex but that is a strength, not a weakness. The balance between difficult and easier moments is handled beautifully, making the journey feel rewarding rather than overwhelming. Wanderer trusts the player, and that trust pays off.

It honestly feels unfair that this game is receiving so much negativity. Wanderer is ambitious, creative, and clearly made with passion. For players who love immersive VR adventures, puzzle-solving, and rich worlds to explore, this is a must-play experience.

To anyone who, like me, is enjoying this game: please leave a review. Wanderer deserves a more realistic score that reflects the quality and creativity it offers. This is a fantastic VR game, one of the most memorable experiences I’ve had on Meta Quest — and it absolutely deserves a 5/5.

Finally, a sincere thank you to the entire development team.
Your passion, creativity, and attention to detail truly shine through in every aspect of Wanderer. Thank you for creating such an imaginative, clever, and joyful VR experience. It is clear that a lot of heart and effort went into this game and it is deeply appreciated.

developer_response.body: Hey - thanks so much for such an awesome and detailed review. We super appreciate it and the support. Will share this with the team, they'd love to hear all this!

reviews.review_title: I'm so disappointed with this version.
reviews.review_description:
I was looking forward to this remake as had original on ps4, but omg I wasn't expecting so many bugs!!
Even after the update I have discovered a bug which crashes the game and returns you to quest homescreen. Should of just delayed it again rather that the clearly unfinished state it's in now. 


Update 16.12.25. Had since launch but can't complete because of a game breaking bug. This game made me quit metaquest. The dev treat you like a free QA service on discord. Civilians shouldn't have to do this!!

reviews.review_title: Please fix the hydroelectric power issue
reviews.review_description:
There is a bug in the hydroelectric power plant that lets you activate the final big lever before completing all the required steps in the control room.

When this happens, the game switches the physical lever to the “ON” position, but the internal “progress flag” (which confirms that the plant is fully repaired) is never set.

Later, at Tesla, the game checks this internal variable instead of the lever’s actual position. As a result:
	•	Tesla reports that there is no power,
	•	the lever is already down and cannot be raised again, making it impossible to restart the sequence properly,
	•	you end up in a soft-lock, stuck with no way to go back because you don’t have an old enough save.

The developers told me that I just need to load an old enough save, but obviously, after all this time, all my saves bring me back to the moment when the bug happened.

Even worse, they told me that I should just restart the game from the beginning. That’s completely unbelievable. I’m a developer myself, and this doesn’t seem particularly difficult to fix.

The bug is a mismatch between the visual state (the lever being down) and the logical state (the progression flags not being updated).

The proper fix is exactly what you would expect:
	1.	Temporarily allow the lever to be moved back up if the internal conditions are not satisfied.
	2.	When the lever is pushed down again:
• recalculate all the necessary flags (all the prerequisites for the power plant),
• validate or not the “power plant online” step,
• propagate that state all the way through to Tesla.

In short:
“If the internal state is not consistent with the lever’s position, let the player reset it and recalculate everything correctly.”

I hope this issue can be reviewed and fixed in a future update, as it can permanently block players’ progress through no fault of their own.

A simple check or recovery mechanism would be enough to prevent this kind of soft-lock and greatly improve the player experience.

I appreciate your work on the game, and I hope you will consider addressing this problem so that affected players don’t have to restart from scratch.

Thank you for your time and support.

Ok I have to start by acknowledging the amazing feat of getting this game to actually run an standalone hardware. Yes there are some bugs- some of which are quite annoying however this is still an incredible game on quest3. Most of the bugs were minor and did not affect gameplay at all. The worst thing  that i encountered stopped the game bringing me back to the meta homescreen( which happened 3 times back to back in the same spot. The 4th try i just moved slowly and gave the game time to process for 20 to 30 seconds and did not encounter this problem again. All in all I thoroughly enjoyed this experience and was quite satisfied with the variety of environments and length of the game. I really wanted to give this game 5 stars due to the fact that I was so impressed with the story/ adventure stand point which stood out far more than any negatives. With that being said, the combat is decent but not great along with the bugs i feel 5 stars might be a little dishonest. If the devs find a way to further optimize and or make combat a little more satisfying i will be adding that 5 star. Just know that if you have the patience to overlook a few bugs- which you should if you are interested in this genre of puzzle/action adventure- you WILL enjoy this game, though you may experience some annoyances. I did not purchase this game until I had the opportunity to buy it at discount because the reviews scared me off a bit. In reality this game is worth every penny of its full price and I would have bought it a long time ago had i known. Thanks to the devs for making this possible on standalone. Im truly mindblown!

Wanderer: Fragments of Fate is exactly the kind of VR game I wish existed more often. It’s ambitious, creative, and full of heart — the sort of experience you can tell a studio poured everything into. The time‑hopping, the puzzles, the atmosphere, the sense of adventure… it all comes together in a way that feels genuinely special.

There’s a real joy in stepping into different eras and seeing how your actions ripple across timelines. The Echo Diviner is one of the coolest VR mechanics I’ve used — clever, tactile, and central to everything you do. It’s rare to find a VR game that blends story, exploration, and interaction this well.

Is it perfect? No. There’s  jank here and there — some sections loads of it. Mostly weapons getting stuck in walls  etc.  or everything juddering but it reminds you how ambitious the whole thing is. The combat is also a weak point. Some of it reasonable at times at other times dreadful-but still glad it was in. Honesty though, none of that stopped me from loving the experience. If anything, it made me appreciate the scale of what the team attempted. There’s also great use of haptics with some fantastic use of music/sound that just give if that wonderful completely immersive feel.

To the developers: thank you. You took a risk building something this bold, and it absolutely paid off. Games like this are the reason I love VR, and I hope you keep going. I’ll be first in line for whatever you make next! Hopefully ‘Wanderer 2.’!!!

reviews.review_title: Great concept but glitches will getcha
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game when first released-initial experience…well you know-wasn’t good.
Playing now and actually getting into it. Used power drill to open a door, left on desk in basement and now need to open vent and it’s gone. I mean gone-no way to get back so now can’t continue game. High price game for such lousy play.
Buyer beware

Yes, this game has some clunky movement and combat is awkward, but the story, voice acting, graphics, puzzles and locations are excellent. I waited for this game to come out all last year and then I waited all this year until after the patches and I’m glad I did. I took my time exploring everything and I got a good 20 hours of game play out of this. The game is massive. There aren’t many games with an environment you can explore that is not derelict and full of monsters and zombies. I can’t wait for the next one to come out.

developer_response.body: Thanks for such a great review, really appreciate it.

reviews.review_description: I really like this game however I have got completely stuck on a level (teprarl encoder) light box in the future apartment and you can't move until you solve it. It's my second black hole capacitor light box so it's not that I don't know how it works but it's proving to be quite demolishing. I have spent probably 5hr trying to resolve the puzzle but no luck. I don't know if it is a glitch so I have restarted several times but know luck. I would have no problem recommending this game because up till now it's been outstanding. For your info I have been playing the game in total for about 15hr so far and probably only half way through and I will continue playing till I get to the end if I live long enough 😂😂😂😂

reviews.review_title: This game was totally worth every penny
reviews.review_description: I love playing this game, it’s so fun and I love the fact that things you do in different timelines changes the future altogether in other different timelines, and the main timeline would 100% recommend it, i dont know why people are hating on this game it is awesome 😇

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the awesome review! Really appreciate the support.

reviews.review_description: Got stuck at the start forgot about it and could not get refund

reviews.review_title: Initially gave this game a 1 star rating
reviews.review_description: The swinging arm movement was hard to adjust to when the game first rolled out so I gave up on it quick and gave it a low rating. Gave it another chance a few months later, now I’m hooked and it’s one of the best titles I own. Give it chance!

reviews.review_title: Don’t be put off by star rating
reviews.review_description:
I hesitated for over a year before buying this - purely because the star rating and reviews put me off. 

It’s fantastic! I’m not sure what people expect for their money, but it is hours of fun and exploring with various locations, detailed environments and a good story line. I haven’t had any of the bugs people have mentioned - maybe the devs have worked on them?

Also huge bonus… it has teleporting for those of us who are motion sensitive.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks for the great review and support!

reviews.review_description: Just Beat The Game And Had The Greatest Journey. Thank You For All The Work Put Into This Game. Really Was Emotional For Me After Slowy Puttting Time Into This Game I Beat it Finally And The Story Was Crazy. TIME itself Is Crazy. Everything you do today is a Butterfly Effect.

developer_response.body: Hey, thanks so much for such a great review! Time is crazy :D

reviews.review_description: I don’t know if it’s because I am playing seated due to disability but I cannot rope swing. It is impossible to get past that 1st one as the rope just does not swing when you move your arms as suggested. I purchased because it said it could be played seated but that just isn’t the case. Wish I hadn’t exceeded the refund window fighting with this game to try and get it to work.”. Waste of money.

I saw this game from a TikTok post and saw a flamethrower water gun watched the whole thing and when I buy the game everything i paid for was locked behind puzzles with no clues and they just expect you to figure it out
So at chapter 7 in the mansion just after taking the 2 people down the game crashes hard, i have selected the chapter and i have clean uninstalled the game it continues to crash. Can someone help?
In chapter 2.2 there’s a elevator and it won’t load, which won’t let me progress

Das Spiel ist nach dem Update einfach fantastisch geworden 🤩
Endlich habe ich, dank update, die Seile ohne weitere Probleme überwunden und konnte endlich ins Spiel eintauchen. 
Danke fürs zuhören und korrigieren 🥰

Das Spiel läuft jetzt richtig rund, sieht großartig aus und zieht einen komplett in seine Welt hinein. Ich kann kaum aufhören zu spielen!

Was mich besonders begeistert:
Der Schwierigkeitsgrad bei den Kämpfen ist frei einstellbar.
Als absoluter Kampfbanause ist das für mich ein riesiger Pluspunkt. Auf „leicht“ kann ich endlich entspannt mitkommen, ohne Frust oder Stress – und trotzdem fühlt sich alles spannend und sinnvoll an. Genau so sollte es sein! 

Die Mischung aus Story, Zeitreisen, Rätseln und Erkundung ist einfach genial. Man hat ständig dieses „nur noch kurz weiterspielen…“-Gefühl – und zack, ist wieder eine Stunde vorbei 😅
Die Rätsel sind clever, die Atmosphäre ist unglaublich dicht und man fühlt sich wirklich mittendrin statt nur dabei.

Besonders nach dem Update merkt man:
	•	bessere Performance
	•	stabileres Gameplay
	•	insgesamt ein viel runderer, hochwertiger Eindruck

Für mich ist Wanderer: The Fragments of Fate aktuell eines der besten Story- und Adventure-Spiele auf der Quest 3.
Ein Spiel, das zeigt, wie gut VR sein kann – emotional, spannend und zugänglich für alle Spieltypen ❤️

👉 Klare Empfehlung – vor allem jetzt nach dem Update!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the awesome review, glad to hear the patches did the trick and that you enjoyed the game so much :-)

reviews.review_description: I finally found out the generator issue. now there are guys and drones you’ll have to defeat it makes me nervous.😰 but anyways my name is Chloe just so you know if you still do want to understand it’s me than that’s fine.

Ignoring the bugs for the first part, this game is great! The movement takes time to learn, but get much easier at the start of the proper game, and the combat and weapon system is just so much better than the first game! And the areas are so much more open, especially in Tikal 1525 at the start of the game. It's nothing short of a masterpiece

However, while there are other reviews discussing game-breaking glitches, I haven't encountered those (at least not yet). There are a few issues I've come across, but often, restarting the save or game fixes it. The only major bug I experienced was at the end of Tikal 1525 where so many assets were missing and I was stuck. I restarted the save from the beginning of the chapter and everything was fixed, but I was losing my mind trying movement techs to reach the chest! However, all is fixed and I'm at the next chapter now

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, appreciate the support. Sorry for the bug you experienced but good to hear you got through!

reviews.review_title: I Want This To Be Good Sooooo Badly!!!!
reviews.review_description:
It has the potential to be an amazing game. It truly is fun to play but there are still so many bugs. I want to give so much more time to this game because I bought it almost as soon as I heard about it. I loved the concept and you can clearly see what the developers are trying to accomplish but it still needs work. I’m hoping the following can be addressed in the next update: 

- When I teleport back to the apartment , my controllers start vibrating and doesn’t stop unless I’m near the ledge outside. But I can’t stand there too long because I’ll freeze 
- Also, when I teleport to the apartment, the power drill ALWAYS is running nonstop even without the battery. I have to physically pick it up and put it down to make it stop. Doesn’t matter if it’s in the vault, I’ll just have to go upstairs to grab it and make it stop. The only work around is to have it in my inventory before I get to the apartment 
- The action for me to press the button on my watch isn’t working properly. I’m always bumping into my real wrist. And it’s almost impossible to get the tool that recalls items. I’ll be able to get to the button but once it appears and I try to press it, either my finger slips off or it just closes all together. 
- Maybe 95% of the world is reactive but there are some small things especially in the apartment that merge into each other. Like the remote controls trucks, they vibrate when I pick them up plus always clip into the rails 
- Most characters look like they’re strung out and haven’t slept for DAYS in the face. It’s weird when they’ll be talking directly at you but it’s clear they’re looking over your shoulder. 

Again, this game is still really amazing and fun. I love the storyline. I know they’ve worked on the combat mechanics but I can’t get to that part to try them out because of the bugs. This isn’t worth $50 right now. Wait until you can get it on sale for $10-15 then the wait for updates might hurt a little less. Hopefully they’ll sort things out and I can get back into Wanderer. Because it definitely has the potential to be awesome. Keep grinding, dev team!

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thanks for your review. We really appreciate the feedback. We've just pushed a patch (Patch 1.5) which you can check out the details for here : 
https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/app/developer-post/1876808373269436/

We're continuing to update and improve the game and your feedback is valuable. I know we've certainly fixed the drill audio issue you mentioned and potentially the watch stuff as there are button based shortcuts for the majority of watch interactions now (left joystick up/down opens and closes echo diviner, pushing X opens and closes watch HUD).

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed the concept of the game and all the puzzles to solve, but sometime the physics are so far off it gets frustrating when you're trying to do a simple thing, like pick something up, and the physics are not working, and you have to fight to get it picked up.  It looks great and has potential to be a great game, if you could just fix those physics. I will increase the number of stars when (if) you get this fixed.
This game is coming together so much now with all the updates, still room for a lot of improvement though but I like the direction it’s heading!

This is a really cool game.
I love everything about it, except the combat. combat is almost laughable, but there isn't much of it.So that's okay with me. The puzzles are really cool. The whole storyline is really original. Definitely recommend this one. I have to make an update. I never collected the remote switch for the bomb to blow up the tank in Germany. Now I can't finish the game. Needs  to be a way to retrieve things that you didn't collect.

reviews.review_title: Much better, still a ways to go
reviews.review_description: So is it finally good yet? Lets start by saying I fully understand where the low reviews are coming from.  It's a super ambitious game, and not all of it worked out.  Visually, it looks rough on Quest 3 and I suspect attempting to put this onto Quest 2 was a step too far. Combat was exceptionally clunky and unreactive due to the overdone weapon physics and barebones enemy ai, and there are some physics related bugs. If all you played was the opening level, I can see how you'd be disappointed.  But stopping there would be doing yourself a disservice.  Wanderer is actually a superb adventure puzzle game, in fact my new favorite of the genre on Quest.  The way the timelines weave around and through each other was inspiring and its so neat the way you need to carry items across time for the puzzles. The included hint system with the watch was super helpful, platforming and parkour sections felt great, and item and puzzle interactions (outside of combat) are excellent.  Later on, once you unlock ranged weapons, even the combat becomes more normal feeling.    It's nice to see they've continued to update the game with tons of bug fixes and meaningful additions like the Echo Vault. And, at around 18 hours completion time with almost no filler material, you feel like you're getting a lot - despite the high price. They're on the right track here, and if they manage to get the combat and visuals further cleaned up, Wanderer could be a top example for premium VR.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks so much for the detailed review, really appreciate the comments and feedback. Good to hear the patches have made a difference and great to hear you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description: Enjoying the game unfortunately can't progress due to bugs in the game.

So, I HAD a love/hate relationship with this game. I got it on release day. Lots of problems. But now, after many patches, I think it is what it was supposed to be. I feel like this is the greatest VR game ever made. I mean... the time travel, the story, the puzzles, a little combat, the beauty of the environments. I was blown away. Still am. I am finally enjoying the game. The echo vault addition for storage was a great add on. The improvement of the NPCs is better. The game is truly groundbreaking. I didn't ask for a refund because I was waiting for the patches.  I'm glad I waited. I'm really excited for Wanderer 2 seas of fortune. It's apparent the devs have really put alot of work into this. It was always apparent. I'm more of an explorer than combat minded, so these rich environments are both entertaining and emmersive. I literally feel like I've been transported to another era, many eras in fact. If I had to equate it to a movie, or something I would say it's part Raiders of the lost arc meets Apollo 13 and a touch of Night at the museum. It's amazing.

developer_response.body: Hey there - we're so stoked the updates have turned your opinions around, this was great to read. Sorry you had a rocky start but glad you've had a chance to discover the true game, thanks so much for the wonderful review. Keep enjoying :D

reviews.review_description: A lot of bugs at first, and quite frustrating… but most worked out. As of 2026, an incredible game, and I am going to miss this team and I am going to miss part 2 of Wanderer Fragments….

developer_response.body: Hey there - sorry to hear you had such a rough time with it. It would be good to get a bit more detail on the bugs you experienced. Appreciate you leaving feedback and persevering.

reviews.review_description:
I was like everyone else, annoyed by the constant delays to release the game and was already mad at it when it came out, but I think it was wonderful. Not what I expected, but it was alot of fun. Very original. I've never played a game like it. While there were some glitches and bugs (like almost all new releases), none that ruined the game or made it unplayable. I haven't played for a while, but I did play it more than once when it first came out. I loved it. Some people are just cry babies and never satisfied with anything. 
I've replayed the game a few times since this review. Since the updates on the game I've had no issues with bugs. I loved the update where they have spots for all the tools now. It was fun making sure I found all the tools, and fragments. If I could give it more than 5 stars i would. I'm baffled as to why it only has 3 stars.

developer_response.body: Hey - thanks so much for the great review. Super appreciate it and love that you enjoyed the game. We also wish you could give it more than 5 stars :D Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: I was going to give this game a low review because the game was half made by release but they did well at addressing the bugs and patching them and after the game is great. Im excited for the sequel

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the great review. Great to hear you persevered and really appreciate you doing that!

reviews.review_description: If you like adventure games, get this game!  I was scared off by the low rating for a long time and took a chance when on sale. Glad I did. This is a full, great adventure. Are there annoying glitches? Yes. Luckily, nothing I encountered was game-breaking. Had to restart some levels to make the glitches disappear and try certain things a few times but always able to adjust and continue. There is a lot to the game. Not all of it is intuitive so I had to look up what to do several times. I don’t care about combat so I played on easy and it actually was very easy and that was fine with me. Can’t wait for sequel!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the great review. Good to hear you had fun playing!

reviews.review_title: Rough Launch, but pretty good now!
reviews.review_description:
Wanderer: The Fragments of Fate is a great game that had a rough launch, but it’s finally reaching its potential. I bought and played it at launch, and encountered a game breaking bug about 2-3 hours from the end that kept me from completing it. Now it's finally in a place where I was able to complete the game!

It’s an ambitious and very fun action-adventure that mostly delivers on it's premise. The combat isn’t the strongest part, but the platforming and puzzles are truly excellent, and the voice over is fantastic as well. There are still a few bugs here and there, and in some areas the performance could still be smoother on my Quest 3, but overall it’s vastly improved since launch in all departments.

With another patch or two, this could easily be a 5/5 game. I really look forward to the next installment in the series!

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for giving it another go, your patience and leaving a good review. Really appreciate it and so great to hear you're enjoying the game. Hope you keep enjoying :-)

reviews.review_description: This game and it's story was amazing every detail was great I actually felt sad beating the game and the puzzles were really well put together the combat was a tad clunky but I didn't mind. The graphics on this game however 🫩 they are so bad comparing this to bonelabs or b&s these are rubbish but I'm definitely going to pick it up on steam for the visual upgrade.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks for the great review. Sorry you didn't enjoy the graphics as much but great to hear you had a good time in the game. Thanks for the support.

reviews.review_description:
First and foremost the game is fun, but the bugs make it ridiculous and at times unplayable. 

Ropes disappearing,  hands getting stuck behind doors, the inventory upgrade rotating puzzle not loading in properly on upgrade 7 making it impossible to complete the puzzle, always hearing a (loud) power tool on no matter if the battery was inserted or not every time I warp into a new section. The power tool one became so bad I had to leave all my tools on the moon just so I wouldn’t accidentally hear
them ￼in other sections. 

The combat is also a bit of a joke.

developer_response.body: Hey there - ah sorry to hear you're having a tough time with this. We just pushed an update a couple of days ago that has resolved a lot of issues and should give you a smoother experience. And the combat has improved as well. We'd love for you to give it another go and would love to hear if it was much better. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: Innovative game that shines after update
reviews.review_description: This game was highly hyped upon initial release, though fell short based on a wide variety of bugs. I only began playing it after the recent (early September) update (the 4th update at this point) and I have to say I'm really impressed. This is a unique game that is really GOTY quality in many respects (the puzzles, the story and the time travel mechanic). It's my understanding that the combat is not where the developers want it to be yet and I am playing on pilgrim mode, which allows me to just focus on the puzzles and story. I love the time travel theme and it's here where the game really shines. If you are on the fence I'd say now is the time to give this game a try, highly recommend overall.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for your great review and your patience while we made some, much needed, fixes! We're so grateful to the players and community for the support. Hope the enjoyment continues!

reviews.review_title: Only in VR - A Dreamy Experience
reviews.review_description:
From the first moment, it feels like stepping into a dream

I was blown away by the updated Quest 3 version!
The improved visuals, sharp textures, and refined post-processing make everything feel alive and detailed. Combat is smoother and more engaging however AI is not to smart and i did not like the knife throwing, but minor complaint because the overall experience feels truly cinematic, with satisfying puzzles and non repetitive. This game doesn’t just look good, it pulls you in. 

It’s the kind of magic only VR can deliver.
I love it!

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for this dreamy review!! We love to hear it and so appreciate it. Hope you continue to enjoy it, everyone's worked so hard on it. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: Oh my word, I’m so glad I bought this game! I’m about a third of the way in, but already it feels like a better experience for me than some of the big titles like Batman: Shadows or Metro Awakening. This one’s much more my style; the environment is so interactive. I imagine this is what Half-Life: Alyx must feel like for standalone players. The graphics, voice acting, and level design are phenomenal, and I haven’t come across any bugs so far. I’m also really happy there’s a little hint button on the watch (not that I’ve had to use it much! I recommend this highly!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review - It means a lot to us. Stoked you are enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description: It suck don’t buy it he glitches all the time it doesn’t let me play it  ￼

developer_response.body: Hey there - sorry to hear you're having problems. Could you explain the issues a bit further?

reviews.review_description: Game is incredible fun, the mechanics of fetching items between moments in time to solve puzzles is great. The voice over is great and atmospheric. Still a few bugs in places and have seen better graphics on quest.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for the great review and great to hear you're having a good time playing.

reviews.review_description: Waited after all the bad reviews on release then heard some positives after updates so I bought the game because I wanted to like it. It's still trash.....

developer_response.body: Hey there - sorry you're having problems with the game. We'd love if you could share what those are to see if we can help troubleshoot any of the problems. You can jump on our discord https://discord.com/invite/znpgRUjwg7  or email us: timetravel@wanderervr.com

reviews.review_description:
Love the game, stuck at the part where you need to get the key and the hook won’t respawn for the fishing pull. Please fix :)
Thanks

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks for the great review. We're looking at this bug!!

reviews.review_description: So I was stuck on chapter 2.3 and I watched a walkthrough of that part with the constellation door and with the way the guy edited it he made it look like the flashlight spawned on the table and he also went right to the door missing the flashlight like me, long story short I’m sorry for the way I phrased my last review I thought I had looked everywhere and even went out into the hallway again to check and walked right past it again thinking it was just a fuse box or something I was stuck on the part and thought I had just wasted my money thank you for your time other than that games great and I’m sorry for my ignorance and the way I was rude in my last review. Also thank you for responding to this if you hadn’t told me where it was I would’ve just continued on thinking that I got scammed or something.

developer_response.body: Hi - sorry you having trouble. Did you look for the flashlight in the hallway outside the main door to the apartment? That's it's original location.

reviews.review_title: Game is not ready, updated july 2025
reviews.review_description:
I was very interested in this game, the visuals looked good. I bought the game on the first day of release. But there is too much wrong with this game. The performance is not good enough, and the controls sometimes leave much to be desired. For example, I have to swing over a gap with a short rope and then grab a fence as a ceiling, so that I can climb further. I have tried it countless times, but I always fall down. Some challenge is welcome, but in my opinion this is absurdly difficult and makes me hate the game. I almost requested a refund. The game developer has released a roadmap for optimizing the game. In my opinion, that is putting the cart before the horse. Better make sure a game is finished, otherwise you can better plan the release a few months in the future. As a result, I have had a bad experience so far, and I refuse to continue playing this game until it will be more playable. This is a great shame.

Update july 2, 2025: The game is patched, I can see that. The performance is a little bit better, but I think it is not enough, sorry. I understand it is not easy, because the graphics of this game are good. I hope the developer keeps on working on that. I will see what is happening in the near future.

Update july 6, 2025: I have tried the game a little bit longer. The mechanics of the gameplay are not good enough in my opinion. The fighting is still a real disaster. I have removed the game from my Quest. I loose my money, because I don’t think I will play this game again. I think I had requested my money back when I could. But it is too late for that. A bummer, because I wanted to give the game another chance. I will never do that again. I think the developer wanted to bring a interesting game, but it is not playable for me.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - thanks for your review. We appreciate feedback in all shapes and forms. 

We have good news for you! We have made a ton of updates since your review. Check them out here.

https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/app/developer-post/1353141716202617/

This includes :

- End to end chapter saves for better replay coverage
- Echo Vault Feature
- Combat Improvements
- Improved performance/visuals on Quest 3
- Improved performance on quest 2 
- Pilgrim difficulty mode that allows for a story like mode with one hit kills on enemies
- A ton of bug fixes

We’re a tiny team with little resources, but we’re working hard—and our latest update makes now a great time to jump back in. For the best experience we recommend starting a new game if you are up for it - A lot has changed since this review!

If our latest updates changed your experience, we’d really appreciate an updated review - your feedback goes a long way toward helping us improve.

reviews.review_title: Should be the best, more stuff than hlax
reviews.review_description:
Should of been the best game, narrative, story, style, items,.. Story idea excellent 

But was buggy, the enemy encountered, like hitting a shoe,

Still a favourite, but very compromised playing abilities

But vr objects great,

Most impressive vr game items and styleee

When's the next game out, sea of thieves or something??

I can do ashers watch voice now, asher this is your great great grandfather, we're going on one hell of a ride again,

developer_response.body: Love the voice, can hear it through the computer! :D Thanks for the 5 star review. Sorry it was a bit buggy for you, another patch is going live soon and should increase your experience. Keep enjoying!

reviews.review_description:
I really had high expectations for this game, having waited for almost a year.
Unfortunately, reality did hit me hard.
First, the story line is interesting and does keep you going. But it ends there.
Execution is so flawed that it simply destroys the core fabric of the game.
From stupid enemies (to a level not seen this century), to performance issues, to memory leaks, to crashes, to subpar models and textures, it’s basically a textbook of what not to do. Play mechanic is clumsy and fighting, bad beyond belief.
After playing games like Red Matter 1 and 2 or Batman Arkham Shadows, one understands the issue is not the Quest 3.
The game is clearly a brute port to the Quest 3, without the optimizations it deserves. It’s terribly unfortunate, especially at this price.
I hate to say this but, keep your hard earned money for another more decent game. It’s just not enjoyable even if the idea behind it is solid.

This review was written after the 1.2 update which is actually less stable than the previous one. Go figure. ADDENDUM - Version 1.2 is plagued by crashes due to memory issues, something that never happened with previous releases.
UPDATE 09/2025 - The developers left me a comment inviting me to try their latest release that contains significant improvements in the areas I covered. First, I am thankful that they took some of their time to reach me. Second, I will gladly give the game another spin to evaluate the improvements. I will  update my evaluation accordingly.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Thanks for sticking with us on this, really appreciate that takes time and effort and we are very thankful. We've pushed yet another update and it should improve your experience even more so hopefully you can have a much smoother play. We'd love to hear how it goes! Good luck :D

reviews.review_description: This game is good, the concept, the problem solving and visuals. I have completed several puzzles but for some reason it doesn't recognize the completion. The hydrostation is completed, but the power lever doesn't work, To continue with the wardenclyffe with Tesla. The Woodrock port a john doesn't appear on the landing to free Jimmy. I'm stuck. I can't move forward. The Nazi gun fight, no bullets come out of the machine gun. So frustrating.  Is there another patch coming up? Meanwhile I can't complete missions.

developer_response.body: Hey there - this bug is now fixed with the latest hotfix! Depending on where your last save was you should be able to go through this and find it fixed. Hopefully that works otherwise it may mean a restart unfortunately. We are trying to see if we can fix it retrospectively but aren't sure that's possible. Good luck and if you continue to have issues please email us: admin@wanderervr.com and we can try and help.

reviews.review_title: They fixed the game. It's great.
reviews.review_description: Message me if you want refer. link.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks for the great review!

reviews.review_description: Im sorry to write this but its bad,i like the consept and for the most part it looks good but gameplay is annoying as hell,updates by the way is 10 gig? This happens everytime you update games on quest it installs the whole game again? Take my advice save your money, thank me later.

reviews.review_description: I loved the game when I started to play it, but I ran into a game breaking bug really early when the fishing pole didn’t spawn and restarting the chapter didn’t fix it. Hoping that’s patched as I really wanted to be able to play the game.

developer_response.body: Do you have the echo diviner? Have you tried echo divining it's original location?

reviews.review_description:
Severely underrated game. I did admittedly get stuck near the end of the game because of a glitch, but hoping that the new patch dropped will have fixed that. Giving 5 stars because I definitely still got my money's worth and glad to see this port hasn't been abandoned by devs.

Edit/response to devs: no it wasn't the hydro station, it was a fair bit past that. I don't want to spoil it for people but let's just say I did a whole lot of shooting, went up the stairs, rode a platform to make some quick adjustments, came back down and then flipped a lever and nothing happened. Every time I go back same thing happens. It will be fun to replay the game though with the quest 3 improvements and I'll see if the issue is still there.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for the your support and great review. Appreciate your patience while we made some, much needed, fixes! If it's the hydro station glitch then yes it's fixed but not retrospectively so hopefully that's not too painful for you! Great to hear you're having a much better experience!

reviews.review_description:
Big thanks to the hardworking devs of Wanderer: The Fragments of Fate. I hit what felt like a progression blocker, but the dev team was there like a shadow—ready to help. Their quick responses and detailed guidance got me back on track. Special shout-out to Ben for going above and beyond with support.

After finally completing the game, I can genuinely say it stands shoulder-to-shoulder with top-tier AAA titles like Metro Awakening. No game is perfect, but what makes this one shine is not just the depth of the puzzles and immersive storytelling—it’s the dedication of the team behind it. Amazing game, amazing support. It was well worth the $50 price tag.

developer_response.body:
Hi there—thanks for sticking with us, and sorry for the silence and the frustration this caused.

You ran into the hydro-station lever “derailment” bug. We’ve shipped a preventative fix so it won’t happen going forward, added full chapter-to-chapter saves for better replay coverage, and improved performance/visuals on Quest 3—so now’s a great time to dive back in.

The tough part: we can’t reliably repair saves that already contain the derailment state. If you don’t have a save from before pulling the lever, you’ll need to start a new playthrough. If you do have an earlier save, load that and you should be good.

If you want help getting back on track, email us or reach out on discord and we’ll do what we can. Thanks again for the feedback—we really appreciate it.

reviews.review_description: I really loved this game . Yes it had so￼￼me minor glitches but nothing to cry about . The

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for the great review - great to hear you enjoyed the game :-)

reviews.review_description: I've played the wanderer for months, in fact I had the OG wanderer game and it's all a must play. but the remake is better then the OG just to put that out there

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! Really appreciate the feedback

reviews.review_description: First time playing this and became addicted. Yeah it has a few glitches but they are tolerable. Hoping there is a new one soon

developer_response.body: Stoked you are enjoying the game! Happy time travelling

reviews.review_description: Ok this is an honest review my second review I played the game for like 2 and a half hour from the beginning of the story on my quest 3 I still keep getting alot of bugs and performance issues and glitches and the combat still needs more fixing is not good for some reason when I try to make a fist one finger stays up from each hand I can't make a fist and the mirrors drops frame alot please check carefully every detail in the game so it can run smoothly the graphics are better it can use a bit more but is good wanted to add one more thing the menu where you start the game please fix it it get glitchy and frame drops alot get to many poppens please check every detail to the game make it smooth for more enjoyment and the interaction  is glitches alot to hope yah see this i won't play it till I see another update that fixes these things

developer_response.body: Hi ya - thanks so much for giving this another go. We've pushed yet another update and it gives a much smoother play with combat improved as well so hopefully you can try again and have a better experience. We'd love to hear any further feedback and if you need help with anything please do reach out to us on Discord or via email, links are here: www.wanderervr.com Thanks again!

reviews.review_title: Incredible fun and mix of genres
reviews.review_description:
I’ll be honest—my first experience with this game was rough. The initial release was plagued with glitches: frequent crashes, getting stuck in walls, and having to restart the app often. At one point, my hand got stuck in an object and I couldn’t free it without resetting. After about three hours of gameplay, I found myself locked out of the Sanctuary and stuck in a strange limbo space. The only solution was to uninstall and reinstall the game.

But after that reset? It was incredible. The game completely turned around. The puzzles are clever and engaging, the world is immersive, and the experience overall is one of the best I’ve had in VR. Once it worked properly, I was hooked.

Despite the rocky start, I highly recommend Wanderer: Fragments of Fate. It’s a must-play—just be patient through any early bugs.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for giving it another try and playing again. The latest hot fixes and patch should also alleviate any other issues you may be happening. Hope you continue to enjoy the game!

reviews.review_description: I really liked the game till I got to the level of needing the RC car for the tank and then from there on, glitches of objects not available or working in the game. Like the water bottle to the water gun disappearing. Constantly resetting the level. Please do an update so the game is playable again.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - thanks for your review. We appreciate feedback in all shapes and forms. 

We have made a lot of updates in our latest hot fix which you can check out here : 

https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/app/developer-post/1353141716202617/

This includes :

- End to end chapter saves for better replay coverage
- Improved performance/visuals on Quest 3
- Improved performance on quest 2 
- Pilgrim difficulty mode that allows for a story like mode with one hit kills on enemies
- A ton of bug fixes

Thanks for flagging the WWII issues. We’ve made substantial structural changes to those levels to improve performance and bugs. Unfortunately, those updates don’t fully apply to existing saves, so unless you have a save from before entering the WWII area, you’ll need to start a new playthrough to see the improvements. 

We’re a tiny team with little resources, but we’re working hard—and our latest update makes now a great time to jump back in. If our latest updates changed your experience, we’d really appreciate an updated review - your feedback goes a long way toward helping us improve.

reviews.review_title: Love this game but I’m stuck….
reviews.review_description: I am seeing multiple other people stuck at where I’m stuck at. I cannot progress the hydro station or get jimmy out of the portajohn. When I drop him and go back he’s not there. Shows the hydro station doesn’t fully have power but it does I cannot move forward there for the Tesla part to become active and I cannot progress anything. So I’m not restarting the entire game because of one issue. So not entirely sure what to do. Love the puzzles, love the different areas and the whole concept is awesome, just wish it was more polished. And like a way to progress when you’re stuck.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - massively appreciate that you are loving the game and sucks that you've become stuck! 

You've run into the hydro-station lever “derailment” bug. We’ve shipped a preventative fix so it won’t happen going forward, added full chapter-to-chapter saves for better replay coverage, and improved performance/visuals on Quest 3—so now’s a great time to dive back in.

The tough part: we can’t reliably repair saves that already contain the derailment state. If you don’t have a save from before pulling the lever, you’ll need to start a new playthrough. If you do have an earlier save, load that and you should be good.

If you want help getting back on track, email us or reach out on discord and we’ll do what we can. Thanks again for the feedback—we really appreciate it.

reviews.review_title: There’s a new bug/glitch in the game
reviews.review_description:
I’ve played through it before and there’s a new bug/glitch in the game that makes it so the drill and buzzsaw screamed to life as I teleport in. I didn’t have this problem with previous play thrus and y’all are lucky I don’t have something like tinnitus. Please fix it. Frankly it was a lot smoother before. 
Also please for the love of everything add in an expansion or something we can do to keep playing after it ends.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - thanks for your review. This bug is super annoying agree!!

On the 4th of September we pushed another update which you can check out here.  

https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/app/developer-post/1353141716202617/

This has a specific fix for this issue but also includes :

- End to end chapter saves for better replay coverage
- Improved performance/visuals on Quest 3
- Improved performance on quest 2 
- Pilgrim difficulty mode that allows for a story like mode with one hit kills on enemies
- A ton of bug fixes

... so now’s a great time to dive back in!!

So let us know how you get on and if you have further feedback please update your review. Your reviews and feedback mean a lot so updating them based on updates and improvements can go a long way to helping us continue to improve the game. 

Thanks again for the feedback—we really appreciate it.

reviews.review_description: The wait for this game has been considerable, stretching back to its original September 2024 launch. One would reasonably expect that the ensuing eight-month delay would have resulted in a finely polished experience. However, while the game's narrative and core content exceeded my initial hopes, the sheer volume of logistical and technical shortcomings has moved far beyond simple disappointment, rendering the game utterly unplayable. Following the most recent update, this is no exaggeration: I am genuinely concerned that persisting through the relentless glitches could corrupt more than just my save data, as crashes invariably lead to complete system failures. Astonishingly, these developers, fully aware of the sheer depth of this digital dumpster fire, still possess the unmitigated gall to slap a $50 price tag on this monument to ineptitude. This exorbitant sum isn't just an overcharge; it's a deliberate act of contempt, a brazen fleecing of consumers for a product so demonstrably, so pathetically unworthy of such a ludicrous cost. Do better ladies and gentlemen just do better!

reviews.review_title: Got bad reviews because of the price
reviews.review_description:
Like I wanted the PC version settled for this version but it was really good I don't know why it got negative reviews other than It probably should have been held back like the PC version 
For now though..
Using the optimizer app and lowering the resolution to 1400 square makes the game run smooth @ 90fps

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks for the 5 stars and feedback. Appreciate your support. We felt we'd priced it correctly for the length and quality of the game but understand some frustrations with bugs. Good to hear you're having a good time with it! (PC is coming soooo soon :D)

reviews.review_description:
Beginning of September : With the latest update I see major improvements. This is ALOT better thank you devs. 
Im enjoying the game so far.

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for the great review and your patience while we made some, much needed, fixes! We're so grateful to the players and community for the support. Great to hear you're having a much better experience!

reviews.review_title: This game could be bestseller  if.......
reviews.review_description:
It is hard to understand how the developer was thinking and  could release the game in such a bad condition after so many delays--horrible textures here and there, bugs, lousy combat and  promises, that it will be better later. It's like/similar buying a well advertised   trip, but in reality it starts with a bus with a slightly drunk driver, with scribbled and broken seats and poorly inflated tires and you're supposed to like the situation.

Edit#1.Devs haa begun to patch extensively the Wanderer...It would be better to wait to release the game with so many bugs and avoid having so many disappointed players. This was an ambitious project and could have been a bestseller if not for all the annoying bugs from the start.
 I also think it would be wiser if it was only released for Quest 3, because the much more powerful hardware on Quest.3 would provide more opportunities in creating such an advanced  game.

Edit#2 Last but not least, I think the developer could reward their loyal buyers with a free and bug-free  separate  mod for the main game as soon as possible.

reviews.review_description:
I have been following this game for years and was so excited to hear it was coming to Quest. But man, there are so many bugs in this game it’s crazy. Tonight, I just tried to play again and the watch just disappeared during gameplay. I was still early on in the Apartment scene so I couldn’t progress at all. It was so frustrating because I couldn’t save in that broken state, so I’m going to have to do things over again. I’m not a kid with unlimited time. 

Devs need to stop releasing these games in a broken state. I hope the Quest 2 isn’t holding this title back. The textures don’t look great on Q3. I hope they release a patch asap so this title can reach its potential. These days, you can’t afford to squander your release window or your title will disappear into the Quest free-to-play kiddie graveyard.

UPDATE: I saw that the Devs posted a Hotfix for this game, but I seriously could not tell when I loaded it up. The game has required me to restart multiple times since I downloaded it. Now I’m currently stuck at the top of the temple because the character on the ground floor is glitched and doesn’t recognize I’m there. ￼￼He keeps repeating the same thing over and over again, and I can’t progress. The bow and arrow has also not respawned in the area so I can’t proceed. After much frustration, it looks like I’m gonna have to start the whole game from scratch. Terrible first impression for this amount of money.￼￼￼ Also, it would be great to have an option to eliminate combat altogether…a novice mode. I’m tired of shooting at things.

UPDATE #2: Is the promised patch live, Devs? If so, do you need to start a new play through to get the new features? I started the game but I’m still stuck in the apartment with no way to get the fishing hook I need to proceed in the Tesla era. It would be great if the developers would update their customers on the patch and at least let people know it’s live. The communication around this game has been abysmal. Removing a star until you provide more clarity and stop ignoring your customers. We have paid $50 for your game. Let us know what’s going on.

We’re a tiny team with little resources, but we’re working hard—and our latest update makes now a great time to jump back in. For the best experience we recommend starting a new game if you are up for it - A lot has changed since this review!

If our latest updates changed your experience, we’d really appreciate an updated review - your feedback goes a long way toward helping us improve.

reviews.review_description: I do like this game. It has its glitches but it’s fun to play. I do like the moon part I just sit on my sofa and look around. My only gripe is my torch has vanished so I could not carry on playing as it’s nowhere to be found, so I had to restart the game  Maybe in the patch add a place in the sanctuary where you can  re materialise everything you have already got through the game so far, One main problem i have now is the game crashes and tells me I have no memory available to play although I have 120gb of free space so I can’t continue it.

developer_response.body:
Hi there - thanks for your review. We appreciate feedback in all shapes and forms. 

We have good news for you! We have made a major update relative to performance that should stop the majority of your crashes. See below.

https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/app/developer-post/1353141716202617/

This includes :

- End to end chapter saves for better replay coverage
- Improved performance/visuals on Quest 3
- Improved performance on quest 2 
- Pilgrim difficulty mode that allows for a story like mode with one hit kills on enemies
- A ton of bug fixes

In regards to your issues with losing the torch, have you unlocked the echo diviner? If so have you tried echo divining it? 

We’re a tiny team with little resources, but we’re working hard—and our latest update makes now a great time to jump back in. If our latest updates changed your experience, we’d really appreciate an updated review - your feedback goes a long way toward helping us improve.

reviews.review_description: It’s good but the combat needs work

developer_response.body: It's coming!

reviews.review_description: The tracking is glitch as hell. There's so many bugs, the graphics and gameplay are not smooth at all, items and even walls that you're climbing keep flickering. And the "swing" action when trying to swing yourself on a rope to swing over onto another platform just sucks and doesn't work. It's such a disappointment to play games like Batman Arkham Shadows or Horizon where the interactive actions are phenomenal and then to play a game like this where it feels like you're playing a horrible knock off that doesn't even function.

reviews.review_description: I was very excited to play this game because of the TikTok page showcasing it. Unfortunately, the game is nothing like it was advertised. The enemies are not only stupid but also extremely broken and not fun to fight. The physics system is mostly fine but it can completely break at times. For example, I tried to pick up a shell for my flare gun, but my game spazzed out and I was stuck looking upwards for some reason and when it went back to normal, my flare gun was nowhere to be seen. I had such high hopes but I can’t give this game more than 2 stars

reviews.review_description: i hab to restard the game 7 time becouse sometimes somthing would not work or i lost things i needed.

reviews.review_description: Must buy!!!

developer_response.body: Amazing! Thanks so much.

reviews.review_description: Personally I found this game fun and glitch free enough to play no problem.... People need to get over stuff not being perfect... Well worth the price longer and more to the game then other cheaper VR games so it cost more ...puzzles solvable but some take some thinking which is nice... Yes the enemies are easy but it's not a shooting game it a puzzle game with a story... Need more games like this in VR

developer_response.body: Hey there - thanks so much for the great review and feedback. We super appreciate it. Great to hear you had a good experience with the game. We have just released a major patch so hopefully any glitches you might have been experiencing have been resolved. Thanks again and hope you continue to enjoy the game.

reviews.review_description:
I picked this up based on BeardoBanjos video, was genially excited. But what came was a glitchy mess. I waited a month to write this review in hopes of patches but it just isn't here. I have videos of the watch constantly activating like a ghost was touching it while i wasn't looking making it unplayable and needing to reboot. There is a lot of pop-in with meshes and textures all over the place, and worse is seeing z-fighting (textures overlap). I really wanted to enjoy this. but the $49 price is a heck no at this point.  (I paid $34 and wish i could get a refund) This is not ready for consumers and is a glorified a beta build.

I want to support devs and don't like writing bad reviews, but I was so frustrated by this game, that I figured out where to go to write this review.

developer_response.body:
So sorry you've had such a frustrating experience and that there's been a long time for improvements. 

We've been working hard to try and capture as many of the major issues as possible and have just released a major patch that should address these. 

Appreciate you providing the feedback and thanks for your support. Hope you can give us another try with the latest update.

reviews.review_title: This game is good but there’s a problem
reviews.review_description: The game is good and all that stuff but the problem I have with it is the combat. It’s really not that good and I was expecting when you stab your enemy blood would just come on the floor and it would be gory but it didn’t happen. Please fix this. Oh yeah and the other problem is the price I had to spend $50 on this game, would you please make it cheaper for other people to play it

developer_response.body: Hey there - glad you're generally enjoying the game. Combat has a little more work to go and it'll never be gory but the game is predominantly a puzzle adventure. There are some sales coming up soon so hopefully more people get to play the game. Hope you continue to enjoy it!
